title: maerua undulata
intro: Maerua undulata, also known as the African cherry or undulated maerua, is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. The plant is traditionally used in African medicine, particularly in West Africa, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Maerua undulata include its potential to treat fever, malaria, and other infections, as well as its ability to reduce pain and inflammation. In traditional herbal preparations, Maerua undulata is often used in combination with other herbs to make decoctions or infusions, which can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the skin.
